talk_metadata.talk_title: Extracellular Vesicles and Particles, Their Biogenesis and Function
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't know why some recipient cells are more susceptible to cargo uptake
Speaker said: we don't fully understand the mechanisms of microvesicle uptake

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of identified receptors on recipient cells that mediate selective uptake of TMVs
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: We don't know why some recipient cells are more susceptible to cargo uptake

key_data_gaps: No in vivo functional validation of TMV-mediated cargo delivery
No identification of surface receptors governing selective uptake

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify and target the unknown recipient cell receptors that mediate TMV uptake to block pro‑invasive signaling.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: We don't know why some recipient cells are more susceptible to cargo uptake
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model protein‑protein interaction networks to predict candidate surface receptors and design inhibitors.

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop biomarkers based on TMV cargo (pre‑miR‑21, DNA) to stratify patients with high invasive potential.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Large EVs from metastatic breast cancer cells induced very robust induction of endothelial tubes
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi‑omics EV cargo data to generate predictive signatures for aggressive disease.

unresolved_questions: What specific surface proteins on recipient cells mediate TMV uptake?
How is the AF6‑GRP1‑Exportin5 complex regulated at the molecular level?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Los Angeles and Sapienza University, Rome
talk_metadata.talk_title: Biology to Translation of Large Oncosomes
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we cannot be sure that isolated EVs are tumor‑derived
Speaker said: we are still working on robust clinical‑grade enrichment methods

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential contamination with non‑tumor EVs and lipoproteins despite size‑based separation

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Large oncosome‑based liquid biopsy for cancer progression
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all patients regardless of tumor type without molecular stratification
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Tumor‑specific surface markers to enrich cancer‑derived oncosomes
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential underpowering due to heterogeneous oncosome signal

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Large oncosome enrichment via size‑based ultracentrifugation
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed reliance on differential centrifugation and gradient steps, which are labor‑intensive and not easily scalable
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: High‑speed ultracentrifugation limits throughput for clinical deployment
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad plasma isolation without tumor‑specific capture may dilute biomarker signal

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that large oncosomes strongly promote metastasis in mice, then concluded that current EV‑based diagnostics are ready for clinical use despite acknowledging contamination and enrichment challenges

key_data_gaps: Lack of prospective clinical validation of large oncosome biomarkers
No standardized assay for routine clinical use

crispro_opportunity.description: Create a CrisPRO‑driven multi‑analyte panel focusing on mitochondrial transcripts and palmitoylated proteins enriched in large oncosomes.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Large oncosomes are enriched in mitochondrial transcripts and proteins
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate proteomic and transcriptomic signatures to design a targeted capture assay with higher specificity.

crispro_opportunity.description: Design a biomarker‑driven trial that enrolls patients based on presence of large oncosome markers rather than all‑comer plasma samples.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: We cannot be sure that isolated EVs are tumor‑derived
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model patient stratification algorithms to optimize enrichment thresholds.

cited_competitors.name: David Liden
cited_competitors.context: Pioneered discovery of exomeres and supermeres, referenced for background

cited_competitors.name: Stanton L. Coffey
cited_competitors.context: Identified supermeres, mentioned as part of EV subpopulation landscape

unresolved_questions: What specific markers can definitively distinguish tumor‑derived large oncosomes from normal plasma particles?
How can large oncosome isolation be scaled for routine clinical testing?

companies_to_monitor: Companies developing EV‑based liquid biopsy platforms

talk_metadata.talk_title: Activated T‑cell Derived Extracellular Vesicles as Immunomodulatory Therapeutics
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't know why some tumors respond to T‑cell EVs
Speaker said: we are still figuring out the exact mechanism of DNA packaging

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Activated T‑cell EV DNA packaging pathway
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Unidentified genes governing EV DNA export (immune developmental genes only partially known)
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: We don't know why some recipient cells are more susceptible to cargo uptake

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Therapeutic use of activated T‑cell EVs
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that the DNA packaging mechanism is not fully understood, suggesting potential reproducibility issues
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Production of sufficient quantities of activated T‑cell EVs with consistent DNA content
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on short‑course dosing (five treatments) may limit durability

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated robust anti‑tumor effects of activated T‑cell EVs, yet admitted that the underlying DNA‑mediated mechanism is still unknown

key_data_gaps: Mechanistic dissection of DNA vs protein contributions

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to identify and engineer T‑cell EVs with optimized DNA cargo that maximizes antigen presentation without triggering autoimmunity.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Activated T‑cell EV DNA drives antigen presentation genes in dendritic cells
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict DNA‑protein interaction networks to design EVs with selective immunostimulatory payloads.

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine activated T‑cell EV therapy with checkpoint inhibitors (e.g., anti‑PD‑1) to achieve durable tumor control.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Combination of activated T‑cell EVs with anti‑PD‑1 halted tumor growth for >30 days
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synergistic effects of EV‑mediated antigen presentation with checkpoint blockade.

cited_competitors.name: Amgen sotorasib
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned indirectly when discussing EV‑based therapies versus small‑molecule inhibitors

unresolved_questions: What is the structural form of EV‑associated DNA (nucleosome‑bound vs naked)?
How does granzyme B facilitate nuclear membrane rupture in recipient cells?

assets_to_track: DNA packaging gene knockouts (e.g., APAV1, NCF1)
